title: ranunculus aquatilis var. subrigidus
intro: Ranunculus aquatilis var. subrigidus, also known as the stiff-leaved water crowfoot, is a plant species that belongs to the buttercup family and is primarily found in Europe and Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Ranunculus aquatilis var. subrigidus include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as dried flowers, leaves, or roots, which can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
